entry.title: Background Job for ME05
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

We have a requirement to generate the Source List automatically. For this  we have created a variant for ME05 and scheduled it as a background job.

The initial selection screen is executed successfully in the background without any issues. However, after the initial screen  ME05 displays a second screen containing the list of records. On this screen, we need to select all line items and click the Save button to generate the Source List.

Is there any standard way to achieve this in a background job? If so, please share the approach. Any suggestions would be appreciated.

 

Advance Thanks.

entry.title: Projecting WBS Element onto Customer Line Items
entry.content:
We are currently unable to view the WBS Element on Customer Line Items within standard operational apps, and we need guidance on the standard configuration to enable this.

During a customer invoice posting (e.g., Journal Entry 9400000112), the WBS Element is successfully populated on the P&L line item (Item 000002 shows WBS 'UAE/ACC/23/WO' in I_ACCOUNTINGDOCUMENTJOURNAL). However, the Customer/AR reconciliation line (Item 000001) leaves the WBS Element field blank. Consequently, the WBS Element does not appear when viewing the invoice in the "Manage Customer Line Items" app

entry.title: Re: Badi for the Stock Information message at PR level
entry.content:
Yes, this requirement can be achieved, but there is no standard BAdI specifically meant to display available stock as an informational message in both scenarios. The implementation approach depends on whether the client is using SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, Private Cloud, or On-Premise.
1. During Purchase Requisition (PR) Creation
If the PR is created using the Manage Purchase Requisitions Fiori app or ME51N equivalent:
You can implement one of the following enhancement spots:
• BAdI: MMPUR_S4_PR_CHECK (S/4HANA)
o Used to validate Purchase Requisition data before saving.
o You can read the available stock using CDS views or APIs and display an Information (I) message.
o This is the recommended enhancement point for PR validations.
Alternatively:
• ME_PROCESS_REQ_CUST (for On-Premise/GUI transactions)
o Method: PROCESS_ITEM
o Read material, plant, storage location.
o Fetch stock from MATDOC, I_MaterialStock, or MMIM stock APIs.
o Raise an informational message.
________________________________________
2. At My Inbox (Approver) Level
This is more challenging because My Inbox is a generic workflow application.
There is no standard MM BAdI that allows you to inject custom messages directly into the My Inbox approval screen.
Possible approaches are:
• Extend the CDS/OData service used by the approval app to expose current stock.
• Extend the UI using a Fiori extension.
• Implement a workflow enhancement or determination to display stock information.
• Use a side panel or custom section to show available stock.
Displaying a popup/information message in My Inbox via BAdI is generally not supported in standard SAP.
________________________________________
Stock Retrieval Logic
Regardless of the enhancement point, available stock can be obtained from:
• CDS View I_MaterialStock
• CDS View I_StockQuantityCurrentValue
• Table MATDOC (S/4HANA)
• Tables MARD/MCHB (depending on the stock type)
• Standard MM APIs
________________________________________
Recommendation
Requirement Suggested Enhancement
PR Creation BAdI MMPUR_S4_PR_CHECK (preferred) or ME_PROCESS_REQ_CUST (On-Premise)
My Inbox Approval Fiori extension or Workflow enhancement (no standard BAdI to display informational messages)
Clarification Required
To suggest the exact BAdI, could you please confirm:
1. Is your system SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, Private Cloud, or On-Premise?
2. Is the Purchase Requisition created using the Manage Purchase Requisitions Fiori app or ME51N?
3. Is the approval performed through the standard My Inbox Fiori app or a custom approval application?
Once these details are confirmed, I can provide the exact enhancement spot and a sample ABAP implementation.

entry.title: Re: Connect to ldap server in CAP (nodeJS) project on BTP
entry.content:
We had a similar requirement, and we couldn't achieve it using LDAP alone. We eventually resolved the issue by switching to Azure AD (Microsoft Entra ID) and using the Microsoft Graph API.

The high-level steps are:

  1. Create an Azure account (if you don't already have one) and register an application in the Azure portal. Make sure this aligns with your organization's security and IT policies.
  2. Obtain the Application (Client) ID and Tenant ID for your application.
  3. Configure the required Microsoft Graph API permissions. By default, we only had basic user permissions, but we had to request Admin Consent to grant broader permissions (for example, to read all users).
  4. Generate an access token using Azure AD.
  5. Use the Microsoft Graph SDK to query the required user information.

The following npm packages were used in our implementation:

@azure/identity
@microsoft/microsoft-graph-client

Below is a sample implementation that demonstrates the approach:

```typescript
import { ClientSecretCredential } from "@azure/identity";
import { Client } from "@microsoft/microsoft-graph-client";

async function createGraphClient() {
  const credential = new ClientSecretCredential(
    "<TENANT_ID>",
    "<CLIENT_ID>",
    "<CLIENT_SECRET>"
  );

  return Client.initWithMiddleware({
    authProvider: {
      getAccessToken: async () => {
        const token = await credential.getToken(
          "https://graph.microsoft.com/.default"
        );
        return token?.token ?? "";
      },
    },
  });
}

async function getUsersByCostCenter(costCenter: string) {
  const client = await createGraphClient();

  const response = await client
    .api("/users")
    .select("id,displayName,mail")
    .filter(
      `onPremisesExtensionAttributes/extensionAttribute2 eq '${costCenter}'`
    )
    .get();

  return response.value;
}

// Example
const users = await getUsersByCostCenter("10001");
console.log(users);
```

This approach worked reliably for our use case after LDAP proved insufficient.

entry.title: Public Cloud - How to post a prior-year direct supplier invoice for an asset when MM period is close
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

In SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, our client forgot to post a fixed asset purchased in June 2025. The asset master is already created, and now they want to record the acquisition with the original posting date (June 2025).

When we use Create Supplier Invoice or Create Supplier Invoice - Advanced (direct invoice without PO), we receive Message M8022 stating that the MM posting period is not allowed, even though the FI posting period is open.

The Post Asset Acquisition app allows the posting, but it does not create the supplier liability.

Is there any standard way in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud to post a prior-year direct supplier invoice for an asset (Dr Asset / Cr Supplier), or is Post Asset Acquisition the only supported option in this scenario?

Thank you in advance for your guidance.

entry.title: Re: [S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition] Multi-approver workflo...
entry.content:
Hello Henrike,

Thank you very much for your response and for taking the time to provide such detailed guidance — it is really appreciated.

I just wanted to clarify one point regarding item 3 (workflow configuration), as I believe there might be a small inaccuracy.

As far as I understand, both standard workflows:

  • Workflow for Blocked Invoices, and
  • Workflow for Parked Supplier Invoices as Complete

do support cost center-based logic to some extent. Specifically:

  • As start condition, you can use “Account Assignment Category ‘Cost Center’ is used” = Yes
  • And for recipients, you can use “Persons Responsible for All Used Cost Centers”

So, there is indeed a standard way to route approval based on the cost center.

However, the limitation I am facing is that: 👉 the cost center master data only allows a single “Responsible Person”,
which prevents defining multiple approvers per cost center, which is the actual requirement.

So the gap is not so much in triggering the workflow based on the cost center, but rather in supporting multiple approvers for the same cost center within the standard functionality.

That said, thanks again for pointing me to the flexible workflow and for the overall explanation — it was very helpful.

Kind regards,

Without MBC

You do not need SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity if:

You receive the CAMT.053 file directly from the bank.
Your middleware converts or forwards the XML to the SOAP service.
You configure Communication Arrangement SAP_COM_0316.

entry.title: Attachments in Quality Notification emails are not included when I receive the email.
entry.content:
I am experiencing an issue where attachments in Quality Notification emails are not attached when I receive the email. I have reviewed common issues and configuration settings but the problem persists. 

One of the functionalities of Quality Notification does not work because the attachment is not sent along with the email. In QM02, you can attach any document using this:

 

 

 

Go to “Attachment List”

 

 

Afterwards, you will see all attachments:

 

 

 

To send:

in QM02, under the Action Box, there is "Send Mail with Attachment".

 

 

Click “Select all Attachments” and click “Yes”.

 

 

 

Enter LOCL as Output Device and Press Continue.

 

 

Put some details:

 

 

And then click “Send” icon.

At this point, you will be prompted with pop-up window and displaying the attachment in the Quality Notification.

 

IMPORTANT: Please take note that we have ALL settings as mentioned in 2564368 - QM02- Unable to send mail with attachment via activity box | How to configure 'Sending Notifications with Attachments'? - SAP for Me.

Still the issue is not resolved.

 

 

Click “Check”.

 

Then choose the partner and click check.

 

 

And then click “Check”.

However, upon checking the email, there the selected attachment from Quality Notification is not attached in to the email.

entry.title: Two Decades of Proven Excellence: Syntax Achieves SAP Golden Certificate While Driving Innovation
entry.content:
How do you keep mission-critical SAP environments running flawlessly while continuously pushing innovation forward? In this interview, Syntax shares how its deep SAP heritage shapes a modern, global approach to operations – and marks a major milestone along the way. By achieving SAP certification for the 10th consecutive time, Syntax has earned the prestigious Golden Certificate, underlining its long-standing commitment to quality and operational excellence. Combined with newly achieved global certifications in SAP Security Operations and SAP Business AI Operations, this recognition highlights Syntax's ability to deliver secure, future-ready services at scale. From cloud transformation to the growing role of SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) and AI, discover how Syntax helps customers turn SAP operations into a true driver of business value.

Could you start by telling us about your company and the services you offer?

Syntax is a global technology solutions and services provider with deep expertise in running and transforming mission-critical enterprise environments. We help customers manage complexity across cloud, applications, infrastructure, security, and end-user services, with SAP being one of our core strategic focus areas.

In the SAP space, we support customers across the full lifecycle: from advisory, migration, and implementation through to technical managed services, application management, optimization, and innovation. That includes operating highly business-critical SAP landscapes, supporting transformation to SAP S/4HANA and cloud-based environments, and helping customers unlock additional value through SAP BTP, automation, security, and AI.

What differentiates Syntax is our Boutique @ Scale approach. The ability to combine strong operational discipline with a forward-looking innovation mindset, tailoring every engagement to our customers' unique priorities. Customers come to us not only to keep their SAP environments stable and secure, but to evolve them as business needs change, backed by the agility and personal care of a boutique, delivered with the reach and resilience of a global enterprise.

 

 

Your SAP roots run deep, brought to Syntax through Freudenberg IT, whose SAP pedigree reaches back almost to SAP's founding. How has that heritage shaped who Syntax is today as an SAP operations partner – and is it still something you draw on?

Our heritage through Freudenberg IT has had a lasting impact on who we are as an SAP operations partner. It shaped a culture centered on reliability, accountability, process discipline, and long-term customer commitment, all of which remain essential when you are operating business-critical SAP environments.

That background gave us a very practical understanding of what enterprise customers need from an operations partner: not just technical expertise, but consistency, governance, responsiveness, and the ability to support transformation without compromising stability.

We absolutely still draw on that heritage today. While Syntax has evolved into a global organization with a broad portfolio and a modern cloud- and innovation-focused vision, the core DNA remains the same: trusted operations, deep SAP knowledge, and a commitment to helping customers run mission-critical systems with confidence.

 

Looking back at those early years of the partnership, what inspired your decision to seek your first service certification in 2002 – initially in Germany – and what then drove the decision to expand that certification globally in 2006?

From the beginning, we saw certification not simply as a badge, but as an important way to benchmark ourselves against SAP's own operational standards and to validate the quality of the services we were delivering to customers. Starting in Germany made sense because that was where our SAP operations heritage was strongest and where we had built deep experience in supporting complex customer landscapes. As customer expectations grew and our delivery model became increasingly international, expanding that certification globally was the next natural step. It reflected two priorities: first, giving customers the confidence that they would receive the same high-quality service wherever they operated; and second, creating a globally consistent operational framework across our teams. In that sense, certification supported both trust and scale.

Handover of the golden certificate, June 2026

 

How would you describe Syntax's SAP operations proposition to a customer who doesn't want to choose between operational reliability and continuous innovation?

Our message would be very simple: you should not have to choose between the two. At Syntax, we believe the strongest SAP operations model is one that protects the business today while enabling transformation for tomorrow. That means delivering stable, secure, and highly governed day-to-day operations while also helping customers adopt new capabilities in areas such as cloud, automation, SAP BTP, and AI.

We often describe this as helping customers optimize the "run" so they can invest more confidently in the "grow". In practice, that means reducing operational risk, increasing visibility, improving efficiency, and creating the right foundation for continuous innovation. For customers, the outcome is not just better uptime; it is a more agile SAP environment that supports long-term business change.

 

 

How do you experience the audits in practice, and what role do they play for your organization?

We see the audits as rigorous, constructive, and valuable. They are an important discipline for the organization because they require us to demonstrate - not just describe -  the maturity of our processes, controls, and service delivery practices.

In practice, audits help us maintain a strong level of operational consistency across teams and regions. They also create a useful feedback loop, highlighting where we are strong and where we can continue to improve. Internally, that reinforces accountability and continuous improvement; externally, it gives customers and SAP additional confidence in the quality and reliability of our services.

So for us, audits are not just compliance exercises. They are an important part of how we sustain operational excellence at scale.

 

For the first time, you achieved global certification for SAP security operations and SAP Business AI operations - can you tell us how your competencies around Security and Business AI operations are adding value to your service offerings?

These certifications are important because they reflect the evolving customer expectations. Today, customers want innovation, but they also want it delivered in a way that is secure, governed, and operationally robust. On the security side, our capabilities help customers strengthen the protection of their SAP landscapes through a combination of foundational controls, continuous monitoring, vulnerability management, privileged access governance, and broader security operations support. That adds value by reducing risk, improving visibility, and helping customers operate with greater confidence in increasingly complex hybrid environments.

On the Business AI side, the value is about helping customers move from interest to operational reality. It is one thing to explore AI use cases; it is another to run them reliably in enterprise environments. Our role is to help customers embed AI into their SAP landscape in a scalable, supportable way that is aligned with operational best practices. Together, Security and Business AI operations allow us to help customers innovate more safely and more effectively.

 

As SAP is strongly focusing on the cloud and encourages partners to support customers in their migration journey, how are you already helping customers in this area - and looking three to five years ahead, what role do SAP BTP and AI play in Syntax's vision and how do you expect your capabilities to evolve?

We are already supporting customers across the full cloud migration journey: from assessment and planning through migration, conversion, upgrade, and post-go-live operations. That includes helping customers modernize existing SAP environments, move to cloud-based operating models, and build the governance and support structures required for long-term success.

A major part of our value is that we do not view migration as a one-time project. We see it as part of a broader transformation of our operating model. Customers need a partner that can help them move, stabilize, optimize, and then continue to innovate.

Looking three to five years ahead, we see SAP BTP playing a central role as the platform for clean-core extensions, integration, automation, and data-driven innovation. We also expect AI to become much more deeply embedded - both in business processes and in the way SAP environments are operated and optimized. For Syntax, that means continuing to evolve our capabilities in platform operations, cloud governance, automation, observability, security, and AI-enabled service delivery. Our vision is to help customers build SAP operating models that are not only cloud-ready, but also innovation-ready.

 

If you could give one piece of advice to a customer who is just beginning to think about their SAP operations strategy for the next decade, what would it be?

If we had to highlight one key recommendation, it would be this: treat SAP operations as a strategic business capability rather than just a technical support function.

Over the next decade, organizations will need to balance resilience, security, cost efficiency, and continuous innovation far more closely than before. Those that succeed will be the ones who design their operating models early on, with standardization, automation, cloud readiness, and strong governance built in from the outset.

Ultimately, it is important not to separate your operations strategy from your transformation strategy. Reliable and well-governed operations are not just the foundation for stability - they are what make sustainable innovation possible.

 

Information on the certification program for operations partners, as well as an overview of all certified partners, can be found in the SAP Operations Partner Guide.

Visit OPC at SAP.com or on the SAP Partner Portal.

entry.title: SSC v2+ ESM = How to Track API Usage Daily and Avoid 429 Rate Limiting Errors
entry.content:
Background

API rate limiting is one of the most common challenges developers face when integrating systems—especially in high-volume enterprise landscapes like SAP Integration Suite, Calling V2 APIs for Extensibility, Side by Side BTP extensions built or consuming in external SaaS platforms.

If not monitored properly, your application can start failing with HTTP 429 – Too Many Requests, impacting business-critical processes.

Previous Article : https://community.sap.com/t5/crm-and-cx-blog-posts-by-sap/rate-limiting-on-sap-sales-amp-service-cloud-v2-apis-must-to-know/ba-p/14024861 

Understanding 429 in SAP Sales & Service Cloud v2 + ESM

SAP Sales & Service Cloud v2 (CNS v2 stack) APIs enforce rate limits to protect system stability.

Typical causes of 429:

  • High-frequency polling (e.g., ticket sync in ESM)
  • Bulk data replication (master data, business partners, cases, products,  sales orders, sales quote, pricing, activities)
  • Parallel job execution from CPI or middleware
  • Extensibility Patterns (External Hooks, Autoflows...)
  • UI extensions triggering backend APIs excessively

To mointor your API usage before it turns into 429 - You, as an administrator, can monitor API calls made to your tenant to track on daily basis.

Where API Usage Happens in ESM & SAP SSC v2

Common integration points:

ScenarioAPI Usage Pattern
ESM Ticket IntegrationFrequent reads/writes on ticket APIs
S/4HANA replicationBurst loads (bulk API calls)
External service providersContinuous API traffic
CAP / BTP appsReal-time API triggering
CPI iFlowsBatch or scheduled calls
 

Where to Track API Usage

Find the API Metrics dashboard in system settings.
Go to your user menu and choose: System Settings >  Usage and Statistics > API Metrics.

The API Metrics dashboard is currently available by request only.
To activate this feature in your tenant, submit a support ticket requesting access using the component: CEC-CRM-CO-OPS.

  • The daily API limit for a test tenant is 30,000.
  • The daily API limit for a production tenant is 150,000 plus 1,500 times the total number of unit licenses.

For example: a tenant with 400 licenses has a limit of 150,000 plus 1,500 times 400, which equals 750,000.

Note
  • For SAP Sales Cloud and SAP Service Cloud Version 2, a unit represents one (1) full licensed user.
  • For SAP Enterprise Service Management, a unit represents a block of 1000 transactions.

Official Documentation on API Metrics : Link

entry.title: Re: I want to understand how overdraft bank accounts are...
entry.content:
Hi fairulnizammohd,

Short answer: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition has no dedicated "overdraft account." A bank account is a single cash/asset G/L account that simply carries a credit (negative) balance when overdrawn — and you present that credit balance under liabilities through the Financial Statement Version, not by posting to a separate liability G/L account.

How is the bank G/L account set up — asset, liability, or clearing account?

It is a cash/asset account — not a liability account, and not a clearing account.

A house bank account in the Manage Bank Accounts app is always linked to a G/L account, with an n:1 relationship between house bank accounts and the G/L account. Two models exist and run side by side: the classic Balance Sheet Account approach (one G/L set per house bank account) and the newer Cash Account model, where the main account uses G/L Account Type Cash Account + subtype B – Bank Reconciliation Account. From release 2202, new customers are auto-activated for the new bank account ledger. Reference: Using Bank Accounts – SAP Learning.

Clearing accounts (subtype S – Bank Subaccount) are a different concept. They hold in-transit payment items and are cleared against the main account during reconciliation. They are not where an overdraft sits.

How are overdraft balances posted?

No special posting — they post like any normal bank transaction.

Payments flow through the clearing subaccount, then the imported bank statement moves the confirmed amount onto the main bank reconciliation account. When outflows exceed the available balance, the main cash account simply ends in a credit balance. Standard scope does not auto-post to a separate liability account at that moment.

How is the overdraft (credit balance) reclassified to liabilities?

Yes — through the Financial Statement Version, via "Allow Contra Nodes" (Balance-Dependent Account Display).

In Manage Global Hierarchies (FSV), you assign the same bank reconciliation account to two contra nodes: a debit balance displays under the asset node, a credit balance under the liability node. A Bank Account Grouping checkbox is provided for exactly this on bank reconciliation accounts. This is a display/reporting mechanism — it does not create a journal entry. Reference: Financial Statement Version (FSV) – SAP Help

How is it reconciled?

Identical to any bank account — overdraft status changes nothing.

Bank statement processing clears the subaccount against the main reconciliation account; the running balance can be positive or negative without affecting the reconciliation logic.

Constraints to verify:

  • The Overdraft Limits tab in Manage Bank Accounts is a Cash Management liquidity control (limit monitoring), not a G/L posting or reclassification feature. Reference: Using Bank Accounts.
  • Overdraft interest calculation exists only within Advanced Payment Management / in-house banking scope, not basic bank accounting. Reference: Finance in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition 2308.
  • The single bank reconciliation account model carries reporting restrictions (no HBKID/HKTID in several reports, German Z3 audit, profit center derivation on house-bank level). Reference: KBA 3216502.

Summary

Treat the bank account as a cash/asset G/L account that may go credit; do not create an "overdraft liability account." Use FSV contra nodes (Balance-Dependent Account Display, with Bank Account Grouping) to present a credit bank balance under liabilities in the Balance Sheet — this is reporting-only, with no posting and no change to your normal bank statement reconciliation.

Other References: 

  1. Clearing process 
  2. Atuomatic Bank Statement Processing 
  3. Processing Rules 

Hope it helps,

Best Regards,

Michal

entry.title: How to Trigger Formula-Based MIC Calculation Programmatically via BAPI in SAP QM (QE11 Automation)
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

I am working on automating the QE11 inspection result recording process through BAPI in SAP QM, and I have encountered a challenge related to formula-based MIC calculation.

**Background:**
In QE11, we have an inspection operation with multiple characteristics (MICs). One specific MIC — let's call it MIC 0020 ("AC Total Yarn Fault") — is configured with a formula in QP03 that automatically calculates the sum of all recorded defect values. This calculation is triggered when the inspection lot is unlocked (put into process) and the result is locked after computation. The field is display-only in QE11, meaning it cannot be manually entered — SAP computes and populates it internally.

**What We Are Trying to Achieve:**
We are replicating this QE11 flow programmatically using the BAPI `BAPI_INSPOPER_RECORDRESULTS`. The defect recording and result mapping for other MICs are working correctly. However, for MIC 0020, since it is formula-driven and display-only, passing the value directly via the BAPI is not possible — SAP does not accept manual input for this characteristic.

**The Core Question:**
When the unlock/lock action is performed in QE11, SAP internally triggers a formula function module (likely from the `QEFC_*` or `QEFM_*` namespace) that computes the summed result and writes it back to the characteristic. We need to replicate this same backend calculation programmatically.

Specifically, we would like to know:
1. How do we identify the exact formula function module assigned to a formula-based MIC from QP03 configuration (table `QPMA`, field `FORMELNR`)?
2. What are the standard import/export parameters for such formula FMs, and how should they be called in ABAP after recording the other MIC results?
3. Does the formula FM write the computed value directly to inspection tables (`QAMR`/`QAMV`), or does it return the value for us to pass back via BAPI?

**System Details:**
- S/4HANA (QM Module)
- BAPI Used: `BAPI_INSPOPER_RECORDRESULTS`
- MIC Type: Formula-based, display-only, result = sum of all defect characteristics

Any guidance, sample code, or pointers to relevant SAP notes would be greatly appreciated.

Thankyou

entry.title: Extend Sales Order with Issue Summary by Using BAdI SD_SLS_END_OF_FINALIZE
entry.content:
Background

In previous blogs, we shared how to use the Cloud BAdI to Update Sales Order in Finalize Phase and how to Trigger Sales Order Approval Workflow Based on Profit Margin by using the extensibility tools in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

In this blog, I will share another real business use case where the developer extensibility BAdI SD_SLS_END_OF_FINALIZE is used to automatically maintain a custom field that provides a human-readable summary of current issues on a sales order, right before saving.

Developer Extensibility BAdIs for Sales Documents

Developer extensibility is one of the three extensibility types available in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. Unlike key user extensibility -- which is configuration-based and available to business users -- developer extensibility requires ABAP development skills and is implemented in ABAP Development Tools (ADT). It allows customers to add custom business logic using released, cloud-compliant APIs under a strict C1 contract, ensuring upgrade stability. 

SD_SLS_END_OF_FINALIZE is the first developer-extensibility BAdI defined specifically for the sales order process. It is called at the very end of the Finalize phase, after all standard processing is complete, so the document state it reads (credit status, block status, incompleteness) is final and fully committed. Unlike earlier BAdIs in the finalize sequence, changes made here do not trigger redetermination of dependent values, which makes it ideal for writing derived or summary fields. It can also return messages to the user and, when necessary, prevent the document from being saved.

For a full comparison of all diffferent BAdIs, including when each fires, what it can modify, and its recommended use case, refer to the SAP Help documentation.

Customer Requirement

The Supplier Company XYZ is a wholesale distributor operating across multiple sales organizations. Their inside sales team processes a high volume of orders daily, and order quality is a constant focus: orders must be complete, credit-approved, and free of supply or delivery blocks before they can be fulfilled.

When an order is flagged as “incomplete” in standard SAP, the system only shows the sales order is incomplete with relevant statuses — it does not tell the user which specific fields are missing, unless user opens the sales order to check for details. 

The Company XYZ wants a single, readable field on the sales order — visible in list reports — that surfaces most relevant issues in one place: for example, [CREDIT BLOCK] [DELIVERY BLOCK] [INCOMPLETE: Payment Terms]. This would allow their order management team to triage issues at a glance without opening each order individually. The standard SAP S/4HANA Cloud incompleteness log does not satisfy this requirement because it is only accessible from within the order itself and does not consolidate blocks and missing fields into a single explainable field.

Solution

Let's see how developer extensibility fulfills this requirement.

We will use the developer-extensibility BAdI SD_SLS_END_OF_FINALIZE to evaluate the state of the sales order immediately before saving and write the result into a custom header field called YY1_ISSUE_SUMMARY_SDH. This field will contain a concise, machine-parseable text string summarizing all active issues.

The BAdI SD_SLS_END_OF_FINALIZE belongs to enhancement spot ES_SD_SLS_DEV_EXTEND and fires at the end of the finalize phase — that is, right before a sales document is saved — regardless of whether the trigger is a manual save, an approval workflow update, a credit decision, or any other process. This makes it the ideal hook for maintaining derived fields that should always reflect the current state of the document.

The solution consists of three parts: creating the custom field, implementing the BAdI logic, and verifying the result in the Fiori UI.

Step 1 — Create the custom field

Open the Custom Fields Fiori app and create a new field for the Sales Document business context:

Field name: YY1_ISSUE_SUMMARY_SDH

Enable the field for the sales order UI and publish it. Publishing the field also generates the corresponding control-YY1_ISSUE_SUMMARY_SDH flag in the BAdI interface, which is required to make writes take effect.

Step 2 — Implement the BAdI

Open ADT (ABAP Development Tools) and navigate to enhancement spot ES_SD_SLS_DEV_EXTEND. Create a new BAdI implementation for SD_SLS_END_OF_FINALIZE and implement the method IF_SD_SLS_END_OF_FINALIZE~MODIFY_FIELDS.

Implement following logic to convert issues into custom field as explainable texts.

The method receives two objects: a dataprovider (read-only access to the document) and a dataprocessor (write access to custom fields). First, skip the method body when called in simulation mode -- this avoids side effects during OData simulation API calls:

IF dataprovider->is_simulation_mode( ) = abap_true.

  RETURN.

ENDIF.

Next, read the header fields and derive the flags needed for the summary:

DATA(lv_credit_status)      = dataprovider->get_salesdocument( )-totalcreditcheckstatus.
DATA(lv_head_incomp_status) = dataprovider->get_salesdocument( )-headerdelivincompletionstatus.
DATA(lv_credit_blocked)     = abap_false.

IF lv_credit_status = 'B' OR lv_credit_status = 'C'.
  lv_credit_blocked = abap_true.
ENDIF.

Then retrieve the incompleteness information. The method GET_INCOMPLETENESS_INFO_INTRNL returns all incomplete field entries, with each entry carrying the technical field name (FDNAM) and the item number (POSNR). A POSNR of 000000 indicates a header-level field; any other value is an item-level field.

DATA(lt_incompletion_info) = dataprovider->get_incompleteness_info_intrnl( ).
DATA lv_summary TYPE string.

Build the summary string by appending tokens for each active issue. Credit block comes first, followed by one token per incomplete field. A SWITCH maps each technical field name to a human-readable label; unknown field names fall back to the raw technical name:

IF lv_credit_blocked = abap_true.
  lv_summary = |[CREDIT BLOCK] |.
ENDIF.

LOOP AT lt_incompletion_info INTO DATA(ls_incompl).
  DATA(lv_field_label) = SWITCH string( ls_incompl-fdnam
    WHEN 'AUGRU'   THEN 'Order Reason'
    WHEN 'BSTKD'   THEN 'PO Number'
    WHEN 'BSTDK'   THEN 'PO Date'
    WHEN 'INCO1'   THEN 'Incoterms'
    WHEN 'INCO2_L' THEN 'Incoterms Location'
    WHEN 'KDGRP'   THEN 'Customer Group'
    WHEN 'KTGRD'   THEN 'Account Assignment Group'
    WHEN 'KUNNR'   THEN 'Ship-to Party'
    WHEN 'LIFSK'   THEN 'Delivery Block'
    WHEN 'LPRIO'   THEN 'Delivery Priority'
    WHEN 'PRSDT'   THEN 'Pricing Date'
    WHEN 'ROUTE'   THEN 'Route'
    WHEN 'VKBUR'   THEN 'Sales Office'
    WHEN 'VKGRP'   THEN 'Sales Group'
    WHEN 'VSBED'   THEN 'Shipping Conditions'
    WHEN 'VSTEL'   THEN 'Shipping Point'
    WHEN 'WAERK'   THEN 'Currency'
    WHEN 'WERKS'   THEN 'Plant'
    WHEN 'ZTERM'   THEN 'Payment Terms'
    ELSE ls_incompl-fdnam ).

  IF ls_incompl-posnr = '000000'.
    lv_summary = lv_summary && |[INCOMPLETE: { lv_field_label }] |.
  ELSE.
    lv_summary = lv_summary && |[ITEM { ls_incompl-posnr } INCOMPLETE: { lv_field_label }] |.
  ENDIF.
ENDLOOP.

Finally, truncate to 200 characters and write to the custom field using SET_HEAD_CUSTOM_FIELDS. The control- flag must always be set to IF_ABAP_BEHV=>MK-ON -- only fields with this flag set are actually persisted:

CONDENSE lv_summary.
IF strlen( lv_summary ) > 200.
  lv_summary = lv_summary(200).
ENDIF.


dataprocessor->set_head_custom_fields(
  VALUE #( yy1_issue_summary_sdh         = lv_summary
           control-yy1_issue_summary_sdh = if_abap_behv=>mk-on ) ).

Save and activate the implementation class in ADT. 

Step 3 — Verify in the Fiori UI

Log on as an Internal Sales Representative and open the Manage Sales Orders - Version 2 Fiori app. Create a new sales order and intentionally leave following fields empty, and save the order without completing it:

  • Customer Reference (header)
  • Shipping Point (item 10)
  • Terms of Payment (item 20)

Open the saved order and check the custom field — the Issue Summary field shows below:

Edit the sales order to provide the customer reference, and then Save the sales order. 

Open the sales order after it is saved, the custom field "Issue Summary" is updated accordingly. 

Conclusion

In this blog, we used the BAdI SD_SLS_END_OF_FINALIZE to implement a lightweight but powerful order quality indicator. By reading the document state — credit check status, delivery block status, and the incompleteness log — right before save, and writing the result into a custom header field, we gave the order management team a consolidated, queryable view of every issue on every order. No batch job, no separate report, no custom Fiori app required.

The BAdI SD_SLS_END_OF_FINALIZE is part of the Developer Extensibility framework in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. It is implemented in ABAP Development Tools (ADT) using only released, cloud-compliant APIs (C1 contract), and it fires on every save regardless of the triggering scenario — manual save, approval workflow, credit decision, and more. This makes it a reliable, low-maintenance extension point for derived fields that must always reflect current document state.

Please stay tuned for more custom sales order type use cases, and feel free to submit your requirements through the Customer Influence Portal to help us prioritize our product roadmap.

entry.title: ERROR IN ACCOUNT DETERMINATION ID
entry.content:
Hello Experts

I am facing an issue related to Account Determination in SAP IS-U FI-CA.

Initially the client assigned Account Determination ID 07 while creating the Contract Account. However during the Move-In process Account Determination ID 09 was selected by mistake.

As a result when billing is executed the system picks Account Determination ID 09 instead of 07.

I understand that the Account Determination can be changed at the Contract level using transaction ES21. However the client has already generated several billing documents and the corresponding payments have been cleared. Due to business constraints they are not willing to reverse the bills.

My question is 

Is there any way to change the Account Determination for the already billed and cleared documents without reversing the billing documents 

If not what would be the recommended approach in this scenario 

Any guidance or best practices would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Re: Difference between G/L account type: Balance sheet ac...
entry.content:
Hi ChinYeeChia,

Short answer: Yes, you can point one main G/L account at several house bank accounts — but SAP's documented design for that is the Cash Account (Bank Reconciliation Account) model, not the Balance Sheet Account model. A balance sheet account is a 1:1 construct (one G/L = one house bank account), so reusing it across multiple house bank accounts sits outside the intended design and carries practical limits, even if it posts without errors today.

Are there two approaches, and which is standard?

Yes — there are exactly two. When you assign a G/L account to a house bank account in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, the system accepts either a Balance Sheet Account or a Cash Account (Bank Reconciliation Account). For house bank accounts, you can specify either a bank reconciliation account or a balance sheet account, and the two types correspond to two different approaches of maintaining G/L accounts for bank account payments.
Reference: Defining House Bank Accounts

In the Balance Sheet Account model: there is a 1:1 relationship between the house bank account and the G/L account — the G/L account represents one bank account and vice versa, and both must have the same account currency. This is the historic approach where you typically create a fresh G/L set per house bank account.
Reference: Using Bank Accounts

In the Cash Account / Bank Reconciliation Account model (this is the standard for what you describe): you create one Bank Reconciliation Account (G/L account type Cash Account, subtype B) plus one Bank Subaccount / clearing account (subtype S) per payment method, and assign that single set to multiple house bank accounts. This gives an n:1 relationship and lets you reuse the same set of G/L accounts for each new house bank connection.
Reference: Assigning G/L Accounts to House Bank Accounts

Is there a limitation if a balance sheet account is assigned to multiple house bank accounts?

Yes, with limits. It posts today, but you give up the per-bank-account separation that only the reconciliation-account model enforces, and several period-end functions then operate at G/L level only. Even though I strongly recommend using this approach as it enables seamless new bank account onboarding and unified and straightforward setup of processing rules.

Important:

  • Period-end runs at G/L level only. When several bank accounts of the same currency share one reconciliation account, FX valuation account determination, the gain/loss P&L account, and profit center derivation are all done at the G/L-account level. To see the granularity of each bank, an additional field is needed to group items for foreign currency revaluation; otherwise, the Main Bank G/L account is revalued by a single JE line using the total balance.
  • Side-by-side is allowed. You control the method per house bank account via master data, so an individual approach for each house bank account is feasible and no big-bang migration is required — you can move gradually to the reconciliation-account model. Note the G/L account type cannot be changed on an account already in use; switching means creating new G/L accounts and moving balances at a cut-over date.

Summary

If the goal is one main G/L account across several house bank accounts, use the Cash Account / Bank Reconciliation Account model (type Cash Account, subtype B main + subtype S clearing per payment method) — that is the supported n:1 design. A shared balance sheet account may post cleanly now, but it is a 1:1 construct; expect limits in per-bank-account balance reporting and period-end valuation. You can switch gradually, account by account, since both approaches run side by side.

Additional references:

  1. Clearing process 
  2. Automatic Bank Statement Processing 
  3. Processing Rules 

Hope it helps.

Best regards,

Michal

entry.title: Re: SAP PUBLIC CLOUD EVENT - Billing Document Created
entry.content:
Dear @MounaAndre,

I apologize for replying today, but I didn't receive a notification.

To add an automatic header text in a billing document, use SSCUI 101292 Set Up Text Determination for Billing Header.

The configuration is based on three main elements:

  1. Text determination procedure
  2. Text ID assigned to the procedure
  3. Access sequence, which defines where the system copies the text from

Process followed:

1. Open SSCUI 101292

Use the following selection:

Text Object: VBBK
Group for: Billing Header

For a standard customer invoice, select the relevant billing text determination procedure, for example:

T8 Customer Invoice

2. Check the Text IDs in Procedure

Go to:

Text IDs in Procedure

In the tested setup the following entry was used:

Text ID: TX15
Description: Billing Note from Customer
Access Sequence: 501

This means that TX15 is available as a billing document header text and the system can fill it automatically using access sequence 501

3. The sequence in Text IDs in Procedure

The sequence number here only controls the position of the text ID within the procedure.

Example:

Seq. No.: 55
Text ID: TX15
Access Sequence: 501

This sequence does not define the source of the text. The source is defined in the access sequence.

4. Check the access sequence

Go to:

Access Sequences -> Access Sequence for Text IDs

For access sequence 501 the tested entry was:

SeqNo: 10
Text Object: VBBK
Text Object Description: Sales Header texts
Text ID: TX15
All Languages: selected

This means that during billing document creation, the system checks the preceding sales document header for text TX15. If the text exists there, it is copied to the billing document header as TX15

5. The sequence in Access Sequence for Text IDs

The sequence number here defines the search order

Example:

SeqNo 10 = first source checked
SeqNo 20 = second source checked, only if no text is found in step 10
SeqNo 30 = third source checked, only if no text is found in the previous steps

In the tested setup, access sequence 501 has only one step. Therefore the system checks only the sales document header for text TX15

6. Test the setup

To test the process:

  1. Create or open a Sales Order
  2. Maintain header text TX15 Billing Note from Customer

  1. Save the Sales Order
  2. Create the customer invoice
  3. Open the billing document header texts
  4. Confirm that TX15 was copied to the billing document header

7. Important note

Text determination is executed when the billing document is created. Existing billing documents are not updated automatically if the Sales Order text is changed afterwards. The setup applies to newly created billing documents

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: France: Register test participant ID with SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition
entry.content:
Hi all,

just sharing our experience as we recently went through France 
B2B implementation on SAP DRC Cloud Edition (cf-eu02).

After fixing master data (SIREN/SIRET 14 digits, clean payment 
means, no scheme IDs on IBAN/BIC) the invoice reached the PPF 
with status PA_PROCESS_CREATED and ACK received (ProcessCondition: 
Recevable) 🎉

However, the SAP backend still dumps with ITAB_LINE_NOT_FOUND 
in CL_EDOCUMENT_FR_INV->PROCESS_DRCC_RESP (line 14) because the 
CASE statement on ls_process_instance-status does NOT handle the 
new France B2B statuses (PA_PROCESS_CREATED, PPF_200_*, 
TRANSMIT_CONVERSION_FAILED, etc.).

Has anyone hit the same dump and got a correction note from SAP? 
We have an open incident on CA-GTF-CSC-EDO-FR but would love to 
know if there is already a fix.

Thanks!
Pietro

Approach to Transfer Inventory for Company that Splits Entities
entry.content:
Hi Experts!

I am currently facing a project, whereby Company A (CoCode 1000, Plant 1000) has split its sub-business process into a new entity that will specialize in that particular business process (Company B with new CoCode 8000, Plant 8000).

However, right now all of Company B's inventories are still in CoCode 1000, Plant 1000 under Company A.

We are in need to move some of Company A's inventory to CoCode 8000, Plant 8000, and its quiet a lot.

Any advice on the strategy for this?

Right now I am proposing the following solution:

- Conduct GR 561 (Initial Stock Balance) in Plant 8000 with the total qty of its inventory.

- Conduct GI 551 (GI Scrapping) in Plant 1000 with the total qty of Company B's inventory as an offset for 561 transaction in Plant 8000.

 

Any advice? Are there any better ways to do this?

Thank you,

Erika

entry.title: Error "No authorization to start the UI for transaction" missing catalogs Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi,

We are currently getting error "No authorization to start the UI for transaction" for the following transactions:

We miss the Croatia catalog SAP_CA_BC_IC_LND_FIN_GL_HR_PC assigned to the following apps and we cannot access (Croatia is not an active country for us):
107024 Assign Payment Type to a Company Code
107025 Assign Payment Method to a Payment Type

We do have these catalogs assigned to the following apps and we still cannot access:

SAP_CA_BC_IC_LND_FIN_SR_ES_PC
SAP_CA_BC_IC_LND_FIN_WT_ES_PC
SAP_CA_BC_IC_LND_FIN_PLN_PC
SAP_CA_BC_IC_LND_FIN_GL_ES_PC

106351 Define Tax Box Structure Type
106626 Define Withholding Tax Keys Specific to the Country/Region
104311 Assign Country/Rgn Specific Withholding Tax Keys to Withholding Tax Codes
104808 Define Batch Input Session Parameters
106876 Assign Plan Categories to a Virtual Category


Which are the correct catalogs for these apps access?

Note: Provided catalog "SAP_CA_BC_IC_LND_FIN_AP0_PC Accounts Payable - Configuration" provides access to "Set Up Payment Methods per Company Code for Payment Transactions" (101044), which is a different app.


Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: Global Running Day with SAP Community - Mannheim

entry.title: SAP Fiori Workflow has stopped working on some edited PO’s
entry.content:
During a second stoppage to workflow, to make edits to incorrectly transferred PO amounts, workflow has stopped working on the edited PO’s. Has anyone else this situation and how did you fix it?

 

After SAP Fiori migration/cutover, we found some incorrect data, so we turned off workflow to make updates. This was successful within our first month of migration to SAP Fiori. We turned off workflow, stopped users from using the system, made our edits, and turned workflow back on.  This worked perfectly by allowing us to make necessary edits without triggering workflow, and after workflow was turned back on, workflow was working perfectly from that point forward.

 

Last week, after 2 months of review, we found more data issues in another 50 PO’s, so we followed the same process as before. Turned off workflow, stopped users from using the system, made our edits, turned workflow back on, however workflow did not turn on for the edited PO's, and we can’t figure out how to get it working again for those 50 PO's.

entry.title: How to Include/Exclude Time Types into Eligbility for Bonus Calculation
entry.content:
Dear Community,

I know that this topic has been discussed here multiple times, but the suggested solutions do not seem entirely sufficient to me.

We have a customer who requires excluding certain time types from the bonus calculation and including others in the bonus calculation.

Example: Employee had Time Type: Military service – exclude from calculation eligibility

Employee had Time Type: Maternity Protection – include in calculation eligibility

I know that when I include employee status = active in the eligibility rule, the system automatically filters out all time types – meaning the employee is not active during this time.

Which would be great, except for the fact that I need to "ignore" certain time types, i.e., include them in the calculation, such as Maternity Protection, etc.

For reference, the customer has both EC and Time Off. However, I went through Xavier's solution and proposal to include a custom field in Job Info and populate this custom field with the event reason for the given time types, so that the system subsequently adjusts the calculation based on this field:

https://community.sap.com/t5/human-capital-management-blog-posts-by-sap/time-off-integration-in-compensation-eligibility-rules/ba-p/13579851

  1. I cannot use a complete exclusion of the employee if they have a certain time type – I need to exclude them only for the duration of this time type.

  2. I want to avoid implementing a custom field in Job Info because the customer has had EC and Time Off for some time now. In this case, it would mean updating every data slice for the given period and event reason that needs to be considered in the eligibility – which would trigger replications to other systems. Furthermore, it would always mean additional maintenance and record updates if a new time type were to be added and included in the calculation.

  3. I want to avoid manual reporting, importing a lookup table, and calculating eligibility based on this lookup table with the number of absence days.

  4. Solution?

Is it therefore possible for this case to implement an MDF object where absence days would be calculated automatically (based on integration) and these would then be included in the worksheet and the respective calculation?

The calculation is well described in this article: https://community.sap.com/t5/human-capital-management-blog-posts-by-members/calculate-yearly-absences-pro-ratio-for-compensation-planning/ba-p/14077102

Thank you in advance for your help or for any discussion regarding the implementation proposal.

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 20 - Compiler messages
Re: Error when using BSUoM on MM to do a outbound task
entry.content:
Hi @Douglass_sepunifei33174 ,

Yes faced such situation like different Sales UOM (Omni Channel DCs). 

Now EWM documentation is good compare to previous.

Copy from EWM documentation:

"

 

  • The outbound delivery item is created in S/4HANA in the base UoM and sent to EWM. It is transferred to the EWM ODO unchanged. The WT is created and confirmed using a stock-specific UoM (SUoM).

  • The outbound delivery is created in S/4HANA in an alternative (not base) UoM and sent to EWM. If this AUoM is maintained as stock-specific in EWM, it is transferred to the EWM ODO as the UoM of the item. If an AUoM is sent from S/4HANA to EWM, it is not used to find a stock which is exactly in this UoM, but the search strategy for picking is relevant. For more information, see Picking.

  • The outbound delivery is created in S/4HANA in an alternative (not base) UoM and sent to EWM. If this UoM is not maintained as stock-specific in EWM, it is transferred to the EWM ODO as the additional quantity role Sales Quantity." Note: Please explore Stock keeping unit of Meaure in EWM, Config in Delivery line item...etc , and see details in below link.

Source: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/9832125c23154a179bfa1784cdc9577a/109386b7df5842389b52bb2ccb66bae3.html?locale=en-US&state=PRODUCTION&version=2025.001&q=base%20uom

"An additional point is that we have the new "Batch Specification UoM" flag in EWM, which can be activated or deactivated via SPRO. However, when it is deactivated, it creates queues in the delivery integration with EWM. When it is activated, only deliveries using the basic unit of measure or a single alternative unit can be integrated."

To be honest, never work this Batch Specification UoM in EWM however I got the EWM documentation. I guess quantity roles (check the document below) or stock keeping unit set up missing.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/9832125c23154a179bfa1784cdc9577a/eba430da2b8b43cea3d455d575db3d55.html?locale=en-US&state=PRODUCTION&version=2025.001

Please let us know if you have further questions...

Kind Regards,

Ajit

entry.title: Re: Background Job for ME05
entry.content:
Automation Limitations in ME05 Background Processing
Only the three options under *existing records* can be automatically executed in the background. The second screen, which requires the selection and saving of line items, **cannot** be directly automated in the standard ME05 background job.

Try a BDC Wrapper Program
To automate the full process, consider implementing a **wrapper program** that invokes the ME05 transaction in batch mode via a Batch Data Communication (BDC) session. The wrapper program would:

  1. Set initial selection criteria (e.g., purchase requisition numbers, vendors, or dates).
  2. Trigger the BDC session, which simulates the following user actions:
    • Populating the initial ME05 selection screen fields.
    • Navigating to the second screen displaying the source records.
    • Selecting all line items 
    • Save

entry.title: Re: Projecting WBS Element onto Customer Line Items
entry.content:
Dear @VivekDutta,

This is standard system behavior in Public Cloud. There is no standard configuration that copies or derives the WBS Element from the P&L/revenue line item to the Customer/AR reconciliation line item. In the journal entry the WBS Element is stored on the G/L / P&L line item where the project-related revenue or cost is posted. The Customer/AR reconciliation line represents the customer open item from a Financial Operations perspective so the WBS Element remains blank there. Therefore the WBS Element will not be displayed for that customer line item in the Manage Customer Line Items app

More details below:

SAP KBA 3346815 - Profit Center not displayed in Manage Supplier Line Items and Manage Customer Line Items app - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

SAP KBA 3465009 - Filtering by WBS Element in Process Receivable apps


Proposal/workaround:

If the requirement is a report showing the customer invoice together with the related WBS Element this can be handled as a reporting workaround instead of changing the standard Manage Customer Line Items app. The clean approach is to build a custom analytical report that combines the customer/receivable line and the relevant G/L/P&L line of the same journal entry where the WBS Element is actually posted. This must be designed carefully, especially when one invoice can contain multiple revenue lines or multiple WBS Elements

More details below:

Report on General Ledger Account Line Items with Project Reference

SAP KBA 3648708 - How to check which G/L account line items have been assigned a WBS element - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

If the business requirement is to have WBS Element directly available in Manage Customer Line Items or other receivables operational apps, this would be a product enhancement request rather than configuration. It should be submitted via SAP Customer Influence

More details below:

SAP KBA 2963059 - How to Submit Feature Requests for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Product

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Innovation Workshop ‘Accelerate Consulting Productivity with SAP Joule 4 Consultants' June 17, 2026
entry.content:
Dear Valued Customer,

The Innovation Workshop focusing on ‘Accelerate Consulting Productivity with SAP Joule for Consultants (J4C)’ took place virtually on June 17, 2026. Please find the agenda for the workshop here.

Please find the recording of the workshop here, the slides shown during the sessions here and the Q&A section here.

The Innovation workshop provided an overview of SAP Joule for Consultants (J4C), highlighting its architecture, UI & Roadmap.

 

 It furthermore provided a live activation of J4C following the main steps from the Access Setup and the validation of the prerequisites to the Configuration Steps and Readiness Validation. The end-to-end setup took appr 1h.

After the setup was completed, the Functional Onboarding steps were outlined. In addition, typical Use Cases and Prompting strategies were demonstrated.

This workshop concluded with key takeaways and further explanation on the commercial model, the availability, the key value proposition and its roadmap and future features. More than 30 customer questions were answered by SAP Product Experts and consultants.

 

Thank you for attending and we hope you benefited from this workshop.

entry.title: Cascading Specification Versions Through Inheritance Hierarchies with SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
HOW IT WORKS

This workflow automates the cascading of specification versions through inheritance hierarchies. Here's the logic:

  1. Create New Parent Version
    - The workflow receives a parent specification version ID as input
    - It creates a new version of this parent specification
  2. Find All Child Specifications
    - Query the inheritance relationships to find all specifications that inherit from the original parent
    - These are the "children" that need to be updated
  3. For Each Child Specification (Loop)
    - Create a new version of the child specification
    - The new child version initially inherits from the OLD parent version (this is automatic)
    - Find the inheritance relationship record for the new child version
    - Delete the old inheritance link (pointing to old parent)
    - Create a new inheritance link (pointing to new parent)
    - Repeat for the next child
  4. End
    - Once all children have been processed, the workflow ends
    - All new child versions now inherit from the new parent version


PREREQUISITES: IMPORTING THE API ACTIONS

Before building the workflow, you need to import the PLM Specification Management API actions into SAP Build Process Automation.

Step 1: Download the API Specification

  1. Navigate to SAP Business Accelerator Hub (https://api.sap.com)
    2. Search for "Specification Management"
    3. Open the Specification V1 API (https://api.sap.com/api/SpecificationV1/overview)
    4. Click API Specification and download JSON format

Step 2: Upload to SAP Build Process Automation

  1. Open SAP Build and navigate to the Lobby
    2. In the left sidebar, go to Connectors > Actions
    3. Click the Create button
    4. Select Upload API Specification
    5. Upload the downloaded JSON file
    6. Give the Action project a name (e.g., "Specification Management Actions")

Step 3: Add Required Actions

After creating the Action project, you need to add the specific actions you'll use in the workflow:

  1. Open your Action project and go to the Actions tab
    2. Click Add > Available Action
    3. Add the following actions:
MethodAction Description
POSTCreate a new version from an existing specification
POSTCreates a single inheritance relationship between specification versions
DELETEDeletes a single inheritance relationship between specification versions
GETReturns a list of inheritance relationships between specification versions

Step 4: Configure URL Prefix

The URL prefix must be configured for the actions to work correctly:

  1. In the Action project, click the Settings button (gear icon, top right, next to the Release button)
    2. Go to Project Settings > URL Prefix
    3. Set the Resource Path to: /odata/v4/api/specification/v1

This path will be added as a prefix to all action URLs and as a suffix to your destination.

Step 5: Release and Publish

  1. Click Release to create a version of your Action project
    2. Click Publish to make the actions available in your processes

Note: When you add an action to your workflow, the Action project will be automatically added as a dependency to your Process project.


PREREQUISITES: CREATING THE PROCESS PROJECT

Before setting up the workflow, you need to create a Process project in SAP Build:

  1. Navigate to the SAP Build Lobby
    2. Click the Create dropdown button
    3. Select Process Automation

In the Create Project wizard:

Step 1 - Objective:
- Select Automated Process
- Click Next

Step 2 - Type:
- Select Process (Build a business process to automate a workflow)
- Click Next

Step 3 - Name:
- Enter a Name for your project (e.g., "Cascade Specification Versions")
- Optionally add a Description
- Click Review, then Create

After creation, a Create Process dialog will automatically appear:
- Name: Enter a name for the process (e.g., "Cascade Versions")
- Description: Optionally add a description
- Click Create

You will now be taken to the process editor where you can start building your workflow.

Note: Ensure your destination for the PLM Specification Management service is already configured in your SAP BTP subaccount. For more information, see the SAP Help Portal documentation on managing destinations: https://help.sap.com/docs/build-process-automation/sap-build-process-automation/manage-destinations


PREREQUISITES: SETTING UP CUSTOM VARIABLES

Before building the workflow steps, you must define the custom variables in Process Details > Variables > Custom Variables: 

 

Variable Name Type Description
currentChildSpecVersionIdString (T)Stores the current child specification version ID during iteration
parentInheritanceFilterString (T)OData filter for finding inheritance records by source specification
childInheritanceFilterString (T)Combined OData filter for source and target specification
loopIndexNumber (#)Loop counter for iterating through child specifications

To add these variables:
1. Click on the process canvas background (not on any step)
2. Open Process Details panel
3. Go to the Variables tab
4. Expand Custom Variables
5. Add each variable with the appropriate type


STEP-BY-STEP IMPLEMENTATION

Overview

 

Step 1: Configure the Trigger

The workflow starts with a trigger that receives the specificationVersionId of the parent specification to be versioned.

To configure the trigger input:
1. Click on the Trigger node (start event) in the process canvas
2. In the General tab, open the Inputs section
3. Click Configure next to the Inputs field
4. Add a new input parameter:
- Name: specificationVersionId
- Type: String
5. Click Apply

Input Context:

{
  "startEvent": {
    "specificationVersionId": "<UUID of the parent specification version>"
  }
}


Step 2: Create Parent New Version (Action)

Create a new version of the parent specification.

Action: Create a new version from an existing specification (POST)

Inputs:
id = Specification Version Id from trigger (Process Input)


Step 3: Filter with Source Spec (Script Task)

Build an OData filter to find all inheritance records where the parent specification is the source.

Script:

var specVerId = $.context.startEvent.specificationVersionId;
var filter = "sourceSpecificationVersion_id eq " + specVerId;
$.context.custom.parentInheritanceFilter = filter;


Step 4: Get Parent Inheritance Relationships (Action)

Retrieve all specifications that inherit from the parent specification.

Action: Returns a list of inheritance relationships between specification versions (GET)

Inputs:
$filter = parentInheritanceFilter (Custom Variable)


Step 5: Initialize Loop Variable (Script Task)

Set up a counter to iterate through the child specifications.

Script:

$.context.custom.loopIndex = 0;


Step 6: Loop Condition

Configure a condition to check if there are more child specifications to process.

To add the condition:
1. Click the + button after the previous step
2. Select Controls > Condition
3. Give it a name (e.g., "Loop Condition")

To configure the If branch:
1. Click on the If branch
2. Open the Branch Condition configuration (...)
3. Add the expression:
- First value: Parent inheritance relationships > result > value
- Operator: number of items is greater than
- Second value: loopIndex (Custom Variable)

The Default branch will automatically lead to the End event when there are no more items to process.


Step 7: Get Current ID (Script Task)

Extract the current child specification ID from the array.

Script:

$.context.custom.currentChildSpecVersionId = $.context.action_get_SpecificationVersionInheritanceSources_1.result.value[$.context.custom.loopIndex].specificationVersion_id;

Note: The context variable name (e.g., "action_get_SpecificationVersionInheritanceSources_1") is auto-generated by SAP Build based on the action name and order. To find the correct path in your workflow, use the variable picker on the left side of the script.


Step 8: Create Child New Version (Action)

Create a new version of the current child specification.

Action: Create a new version from an existing specification (POST)

Inputs:
id = currentChildSpecVersionId (Custom Variable)


Step 9: Filter with Source and Target Spec (Script Task)

Build a combined filter to find the specific inheritance record between the new child version and the parent.

Script:

var specId = $.context.action_post_postSpecificationVer__ceV1CopyToNewVersion_2.result.id;
var filter = " and specificationVersion_id eq " + specId;
$.context.custom.childInheritanceFilter = $.context.custom.parentInheritanceFilter + filter;


Step 10: Get Child Inheritance Relationships (Action)

Retrieve the specific inheritance record for the newly created child version.

Action: Returns a list of inheritance relationships between specification versions (GET)

Inputs:
$filter = childInheritanceFilter (Custom Variable)


Step 11: Delete Old Inheritance (Action)

Remove the old inheritance link (which still points to the old parent version).

Action: Deletes a single inheritance relationship between specification versions (DELETE)

Inputs:
id = Child Inheritance Relationships > result > value (Item 1) > id

Note: "Item 1" refers to the first element in the array (index 0). Since our filter is specific, this array should contain exactly one result.


Step 12: Create New Inheritance (Action)

Create a new inheritance link pointing to the new parent version.

Action: Creates a single inheritance relationship between specification versions (POST)

Inputs:
id = (leave empty - auto-generated)
inheritanceTemplate_id = Child Inheritance Relationships > result > value (Item 1) > inheritanceTemplate_id
sourceSpecificationVersion_id = Parent New Version > result > id
specificationVersion_id = Child New Version > result > id


Step 13: Increment Loop Variable (Script Task)

Move to the next child specification.

Script:

$.context.custom.loopIndex += 1;


Step 14: Go to Condition

Loop back to the condition check to process the next child specification, or exit if all children have been processed.

To add the Go to control:
1. Click the + button after the Increment Loop Variable step
2. Select Controls > Go to Step
3. In the configuration panel, select the target step: Loop Condition (from Step 6)

This creates the loop - after processing each child specification, the workflow returns to the condition to check if there are more children to process.
